meta.title: Comodoro Py Deep Research — Judicial Power Concentration in Argentina
meta.methodology: WebSearch across Argentine and international media (LA NACION, Infobae, Chequeado, Pagina12, Perfil, Ambito, Buenos Aires Herald, Buenos Aires Times, NBC News, Human Rights Watch, CELS, ACIJ, Revista Anfibia), government sources (argentina.gob.ar, boletinoficial.gob.ar, pjn.gov.ar), and academic/legal sources (UTDT, INECIP)
meta.scope: Deep dive into Comodoro Py structural power, Lijo corruption case details, Rafecas and AMIA/Nisman, Consejo de la Magistratura appointment mechanics, judicial-corporate revolving door, Menem-era court design

findings.role: Federal Judge (Juzgado Federal 4, Comodoro Py); designated to Supreme Court by Decree 137/2025
findings.evidence_es: Segun informe de ACIJ, Lijo tuvo 89 causas de corrupcion y solo elevo 14 a juicio oral. De las 89, 26 continuan en instruccion, y 13 de estas llevan mas de 10 anos abiertas. Tres causas llevan mas de 15 anos: la causa Ruckauf (17 anos), la causa de vaciamiento y expropiacion de YPF (18 anos), y la causa Siemens (26 anos abierta). Fue denunciado al Consejo de la Magistratura 32 veces en 20 anos segun ACIJ, y tiene 3 investigaciones disciplinarias pendientes. El Senado rechazo su pliego con 43 votos en contra en abril 2025.
findings.evidence_en: According to an ACIJ report, Lijo handled 89 corruption cases but only sent 14 to oral trial. Of the 89, 26 remain in investigation phase, and 13 of these have been open for over 10 years. Three cases exceed 15 years: the Ruckauf case (17 years), the YPF asset-stripping case (18 years), and the Siemens case (26 years open). He was reported to the Magistrate Council 32 times in 20 years per ACIJ, and has 3 pending disciplinary investigations. The Senate rejected his candidacy with 43 votes against in April 2025.

new_entities.name: ACIJ (Asociacion Civil por la Igualdad y la Justicia)
new_entities.description: Legal watchdog that documented Lijo's corruption case performance

new_entities.name: Causa Siemens
new_entities.description: Corruption case involving Siemens bribery, open 26 years under Lijo

new_entities.name: Causa Ruckauf
new_entities.description: Case against former governor Ruckauf, open 17 years under Lijo

new_entities.name: Causa YPF (vaciamiento)
new_entities.description: YPF asset-stripping case, open 18 years under Lijo

findings.role: Federal Judge, Comodoro Py — multi-court control
findings.evidence_es: En septiembre de 2023, Lijo quedo a cargo simultaneamente de tres juzgados federales en Comodoro Py: su propio Juzgado Federal 4, mas los Juzgados 12 y 6 (este ultimo vacante desde la jubilacion de Canicoba Corral en 2020). Esta concentracion de poder es inedita — un solo juez controlando el 25% de los juzgados federales de primera instancia de Buenos Aires. Lijo fue descrito por La Nacion como 'el hombre fuerte de Comodoro Py'. Su caso emblematico es el procesamiento de Amado Boudou en la causa Ciccone.
findings.evidence_en: In September 2023, Lijo simultaneously controlled three federal courts at Comodoro Py: his own Federal Court 4, plus Courts 12 and 6 (the latter vacant since Canicoba Corral's retirement in 2020). This concentration of power is unprecedented — one judge controlling 25% of Buenos Aires's first-instance federal courts. La Nacion described Lijo as 'the strongman of Comodoro Py'. His emblematic case is the prosecution of Vice President Amado Boudou in the Ciccone case.

new_entities.name: Amado Boudou
new_entities.role: Former Vice President, processed by Lijo in Ciccone case

new_entities.name: Causa Ciccone
new_entities.description: Corruption case in which Lijo sent VP Boudou to prison

findings.entity: Human Rights Watch / CELS / International community
findings.evidence_es: Human Rights Watch publico dos comunicados pidiendo reconsiderar la nominacion de Lijo (mayo 2024) y denunciando que la designacion por decreto socava la independencia judicial (diciembre 2024, febrero 2025). CELS (Centro de Estudios Legales y Sociales) se opuso formalmente a las nominaciones de Lijo y Garcia-Mansilla. NBC News titulo que Milei 'prometio combatir la corrupcion y luego sorprendio con su eleccion para la Corte Suprema'. Buenos Aires Herald analizo por que Lijo es una eleccion controversial. Asociaciones de abogados, uniones empresariales y organizaciones de derechos humanos expresaron preocupacion. Senadores de todo el espectro politico buscarian negociar votos por Lijo a cambio de cerrar causas de corrupcion contra funcionarios cercanos.
findings.evidence_en: Human Rights Watch issued two statements calling to reconsider Lijo's nomination (May 2024) and denouncing the decree appointment as undermining judicial independence (Dec 2024, Feb 2025). CELS (Center for Legal and Social Studies) formally opposed the Lijo and Garcia-Mansilla nominations. NBC News headlined that Milei 'promised to quash corruption, then shocked with his Supreme Court pick'. Buenos Aires Herald analyzed why Lijo is a controversial choice. Bar associations, business unions, and human rights organizations expressed concern. Senators across the political spectrum were allegedly seeking to trade votes for Lijo in exchange for closing corruption investigations against allies.

new_entities.name: Human Rights Watch
new_entities.description: International human rights org that opposed Lijo nomination

new_entities.name: CELS (Centro de Estudios Legales y Sociales)
new_entities.description: Argentine legal/human rights org that opposed Lijo and Garcia-Mansilla nominations

findings.role: Federal Judge — Correo Argentino case delays
findings.entity: Correo Argentino SA / Grupo Socma / Familia Macri
findings.evidence_es: La causa Correo Argentino investiga irregularidades en un acuerdo alcanzado por Correo Argentino SA (empresa del Grupo Macri) con el Estado en 2016, durante el gobierno de Cambiemos. El arreglo evitaba que Grupo Socma pagara la actualizacion de deuda acumulada desde 1997, a cambio de pagos semestrales de $51,6 millones. Un fiscal comercial considero el acuerdo 'perjudicial para el Estado'. Lijo demoro mas de 4 anos en citar a indagatoria al principal imputado. Pidio un estudio contable con 3 meses de retraso. Fuentes judiciales confirmaron: 'No hay avances'. La causa cumple 20 anos y los Macri aun no pagaron.
findings.evidence_en: The Correo Argentino case investigates irregularities in an agreement between Correo Argentino SA (Macri Group company) and the State in 2016 during the Cambiemos government. The deal avoided Grupo Socma from paying accumulated debt since 1997, in exchange for semiannual payments of $51.6M pesos. A commercial prosecutor considered the deal 'harmful to the State'. Lijo delayed over 4 years calling the main defendant to interrogation. He ordered an accounting study 3 months late. Judicial sources confirmed: 'There are no developments'. The case has been running for 20 years and the Macris still have not paid.

new_entities.name: Correo Argentino SA
new_entities.description: Postal company controlled by Macri family, subject of debt restructuring scandal

new_entities.name: Grupo Socma
new_entities.description: Macri family holding company that controlled Correo Argentino

new_entities.name: Oscar Aguad
new_entities.role: Former Communications Minister (Cambiemos), investigated in Correo case

new_entities.name: Juan Manuel Mocoroa
new_entities.role: Former head of Legal Affairs, investigated in Correo case

findings.entity: Declaracion Jurada / Hudson property / Magali Mazzuca
findings.evidence_es: En su declaracion jurada (periodo fiscal 2022), Lijo declaro bienes inmuebles por $10,3 millones (valor fiscal) consistentes en un lote en Hudson (Berazategui), adquirido en 2010, y mejoras realizadas desde 2011. Declaro ahorros de $11 millones y US$40.000. Comparte la propiedad de Hudson con su ex pareja Magali Mazzuca, quien trabaja como relatora en la Corte Suprema de Justicia. Mientras tanto, habita un departamento de lujo en Avenida Alvear (a la altura de Ayacucho), en el 5to piso de un edificio de lujo, con valor de mercado de US$2.000.000, propiedad de Carlos Bettini (ex embajador en Espana durante los tres gobiernos kirchneristas). Fue denunciado en 2018 por Elisa Carrio por asociacion ilicita, lavado de dinero, coimas y trafico de influencias, pero fue sobreseido.
findings.evidence_en: In his sworn statement (fiscal year 2022), Lijo declared real estate worth $10.3M pesos (fiscal value) consisting of a lot in Hudson (Berazategui) acquired in 2010 with improvements since 2011. He declared savings of $11M pesos and US$40,000. He co-owns the Hudson property with his ex-partner Magali Mazzuca, who works as a law clerk at the Supreme Court. Meanwhile, he inhabits a luxury apartment on Alvear Avenue (near Ayacucho), 5th floor, market value ~US$2M, owned by Carlos Bettini (former ambassador to Spain under three Kirchnerist governments). He was accused in 2018 by Elisa Carrio of criminal association, money laundering, bribery, and influence peddling, but was acquitted.

new_entities.name: Magali Mazzuca
new_entities.role: Ex-partner of Judge Lijo, law clerk at the Supreme Court, co-owner of Hudson property

new_entities.name: Elisa Carrio
new_entities.role: Former congresswoman who filed criminal complaint against Lijo in 2018

findings.role: Federal Judge (Juzgado Federal 3, Comodoro Py, since 2004)
findings.evidence_es: Rafecas fue designado juez federal en 2004 por concurso publico. En febrero 2015, desestimo la denuncia del fiscal Alberto Nisman contra la presidenta Cristina Kirchner, el canciller Hector Timerman y otros funcionarios por presunto encubrimiento de los iranies acusados del atentado a la AMIA. Resolvio la desestimacion por 'inexistencia de delito', sin tomar medidas de prueba. La Camara Federal (Sala I) confirmo la decision por 2 votos a 1. Sin embargo, el Consejo de la Magistratura le impuso una sancion disciplinaria por 'falta de decoro' en su actuacion en la investigacion. Nisman habia presentado la denuncia dias antes de aparecer muerto con un disparo en la cabeza en su departamento de Buenos Aires, bajo circunstancias que aun se investigan.
findings.evidence_en: Rafecas was appointed federal judge in 2004 by public competition. In February 2015, he dismissed prosecutor Alberto Nisman's complaint against President Cristina Kirchner, Foreign Minister Hector Timerman, and other officials for alleged cover-up of Iranians accused of the AMIA bombing. He resolved the dismissal for 'nonexistence of a crime' without taking evidentiary measures. The Federal Chamber (Room I) confirmed the decision 2-1. However, the Magistrate Council imposed a disciplinary sanction for 'lack of decorum' in his handling of the investigation. Nisman had filed the complaint days before being found dead with a gunshot wound to the head in his Buenos Aires apartment, under circumstances still being investigated.

new_entities.name: Alberto Nisman
new_entities.role: Special prosecutor for AMIA case, found dead Jan 2015 after filing complaint against CFK

new_entities.name: DAIA (Delegacion de Asociaciones Israelitas Argentinas)
new_entities.description: Jewish community organization that petitioned to reopen Nisman investigation

new_entities.name: AMIA (Asociacion Mutual Israelita Argentina)
new_entities.description: Jewish community center bombed in 1994 attack (85 dead), central case in Argentine judicial history

findings.role: Candidate for Procurador General de la Nacion
findings.entity: Procuracion General de la Nacion / Senado
findings.evidence_es: Alberto Fernandez oficializo la postulacion de Rafecas como Procurador General de la Nacion en enero 2020. Rafecas declaro que no asumira si se modifica la exigencia de dos tercios del Senado para su designacion. Su pliego permanecio en el Senado durante mas de 3 anos sin tratamiento. El puesto esta vacante de hecho desde 2017, con Eduardo Casal como procurador interino desde la renuncia de Alejandra Gils Carbo. Rafecas eventualmente se distancio de la candidatura. Esto deja a la Procuracion General — la cabeza del Ministerio Publico Fiscal, que dirige a todos los fiscales federales — en manos de un interino sin legitimidad democratica plena, debilitando la persecucion penal federal.
findings.evidence_en: Alberto Fernandez officially nominated Rafecas as Attorney General in January 2020. Rafecas declared he would not accept if the two-thirds Senate requirement for his appointment is modified. His nomination remained in the Senate for over 3 years without being considered. The position has been effectively vacant since 2017, with Eduardo Casal as interim attorney general since Alejandra Gils Carbo's resignation. Rafecas eventually distanced himself from the candidacy. This leaves the Attorney General's Office — head of the Public Prosecutor's Ministry directing all federal prosecutors — in the hands of an interim without full democratic legitimacy, weakening federal criminal prosecution.

new_entities.name: Eduardo Casal
new_entities.role: Interim Attorney General since 2017, lacking full democratic legitimacy

new_entities.name: Alejandra Gils Carbo
new_entities.role: Former Attorney General who resigned, creating vacancy filled by Casal

new_entities.name: Procuracion General de la Nacion
new_entities.description: Head of the Public Prosecutor's Ministry, directs all federal prosecutors

findings.role: Institutional analysis: Comodoro Py as designed power structure
findings.evidence_es: Comodoro Py 2002 alberga los 12 Juzgados Federales de primera instancia, la Camara Federal de Apelaciones, Tribunales Orales Federales, Tribunales Orales en lo Penal Economico, defensorias, fiscalias y la Camara Federal de Casacion Penal. 60 jueces de primera instancia y camaristas trabajan alli — el 6% de los jueces federales del pais — pero concentran todas las causas de corrupcion de la administracion central. Carlos Menem reformo la justicia federal en los anos 90, aumentando los juzgados de 6 a 12 y posicionando jueces y fiscales de confianza. Menem 'diseno' Comodoro Py, la Camara de Casacion Federal y la Camara Federal a su conveniencia. El libro 'Poderosos' examino la historia de 13 jueces de Comodoro Py y su relacion de conveniencia con la politica desde los 90.
findings.evidence_en: Comodoro Py 2002 houses the 12 Federal Courts of first instance, the Federal Court of Appeals, Federal Oral Tribunals, Economic Criminal Oral Tribunals, public defense offices, prosecutors, and the Federal Criminal Cassation Chamber. 60 first-instance judges and appeals court judges work there — 6% of the country's federal judges — but they concentrate all corruption cases involving central administration. Carlos Menem reformed the federal justice system in the 1990s, increasing courts from 6 to 12 and positioning trusted judges and prosecutors. Menem 'designed' Comodoro Py, the Federal Cassation Chamber, and the Federal Chamber to his advantage. The book 'Poderosos' examined the history of 13 Comodoro Py judges and their convenience-based relationship with politics since the 90s.

new_entities.name: Carlos Menem
new_entities.role: President (1989-1999) who expanded and designed Comodoro Py system from 6 to 12 courts

new_entities.name: 'Poderosos' (book)
new_entities.description: Book examining 13 Comodoro Py judges' relationships with political power since the 1990s

findings.evidence_es: A partir del 11 de agosto de 2025, el nuevo Codigo Procesal Penal Federal (sistema acusatorio) se implementa en la justicia federal de Comodoro Py y la justicia penal economica. La reforma transfiere el poder de investigacion de los jueces federales a los fiscales. Los jueces pasan a ser jueces de garantias que controlan el proceso pero no investigan. Se unifican los fueros Criminal y Correccional Federal (Comodoro Py) con el Penal Economico en un 'megafuero' Penal Federal. INECIP califica la reforma como 'un punto de inflexion' que 'altera el ecosistema sobre el que se basa el poder de Comodoro Py'. Los jueces de Comodoro Py se oponen al cambio y estiman un costo adicional de $5.000 millones por ano.
findings.evidence_en: Starting August 11, 2025, the new Federal Criminal Procedure Code (accusatory system) takes effect in Comodoro Py federal justice and economic criminal justice. The reform transfers investigative power from federal judges to prosecutors. Judges become guarantee judges who oversee the process but do not investigate. Criminal and Correctional Federal courts (Comodoro Py) are unified with Economic Criminal courts into a Federal Criminal 'mega-jurisdiction'. INECIP characterizes the reform as 'an inflection point' that 'alters the ecosystem on which Comodoro Py's power is based'. Comodoro Py judges oppose the change and estimate an additional cost of $5 billion pesos per year.

new_entities.name: INECIP (Instituto de Estudios Comparados en Ciencias Penales y Sociales)
new_entities.description: Criminal justice research institute that analyzed Comodoro Py reform implications

findings.role: Current composition: key judges and vacancies at Comodoro Py
findings.evidence_es: Composicion parcial de los juzgados federales de Comodoro Py (datos de 2022-2024): Juzgado 1: Maria Romilda Servini; Juzgado 2: Sebastian Roberto Ramos; Juzgado 3: Daniel Eduardo Rafecas; Juzgado 4: Ariel Lijo (tambien a cargo interino de Juzgados 6 y 12 en 2023); Juzgado 8: Marcelo Pedro Hernan Martinez de Giorgi; Juzgado 10: Julian Ercolini. Ercolini y el fiscal Taiano concentran las causas mas sensibles. Servini es la jueza mas antigua del fuero. Multiples juzgados con vacantes llenan con jueces subrogantes, debilitando la independencia.
findings.evidence_en: Partial composition of Comodoro Py federal courts (2022-2024 data): Court 1: Maria Romilda Servini; Court 2: Sebastian Roberto Ramos; Court 3: Daniel Eduardo Rafecas; Court 4: Ariel Lijo (also interim charge of Courts 6 and 12 in 2023); Court 8: Marcelo Pedro Hernan Martinez de Giorgi; Court 10: Julian Ercolini. Ercolini and prosecutor Taiano concentrate the most sensitive cases. Servini is the longest-serving judge. Multiple courts with vacancies are filled with substitute judges, weakening independence.

new_entities.name: Maria Romilda Servini
new_entities.role: Federal Judge, Court 1, longest-serving at Comodoro Py

new_entities.name: Marcelo Pedro Hernan Martinez de Giorgi
Taiano
new_entities.role: Federal prosecutor who concentrates most sensitive cases alongside Ercolini

findings.role: Nepotism pattern: family ties within Comodoro Py
findings.evidence_es: Chequeado documento las relaciones de parentesco dentro de la Justicia Federal de Comodoro Py, revelando un patron sistemico de contratacion de familiares de jueces y funcionarios judiciales. Las familias judiciales perpetuan redes de lealtad personal que dificultan la independencia y generan un sistema cerrado donde el acceso depende de conexiones familiares mas que del merito. El Consejo de la Magistratura, que deberia controlar estas practicas, esta dominado por legisladores (40% del cuerpo) con intereses politicos propios.
findings.evidence_en: Chequeado documented kinship relationships within the Federal Justice system at Comodoro Py, revealing a systemic pattern of hiring relatives of judges and judicial officials. Judicial families perpetuate networks of personal loyalty that hinder independence and create a closed system where access depends on family connections rather than merit. The Magistrate Council, which should oversee these practices, is dominated by legislators (40% of the body) with their own political interests.

findings.entity: Consejo de la Magistratura / Senado / Poder Ejecutivo
findings.evidence_es: El proceso de seleccion de jueces federales inferiores: (1) El Consejo de la Magistratura abre concurso publico, (2) toma examen escrito a postulantes que cumplen requisitos constitucionales, (3) evalua antecedentes personales, (4) elabora terna vinculante de 3 candidatos, (5) el Presidente de la Nacion elige de la terna, (6) el Senado otorga acuerdo en sesion publica. Para jueces de la Corte Suprema, el proceso es diferente: el Presidente propone, se requiere acuerdo de 2/3 del Senado, con audiencias publicas. En la practica, Milei intento evadir este requisito con el Decreto 137/2025, designando a Lijo y Garcia-Mansilla 'en comision' sin acuerdo del Senado, lo que fue declarado inconstitucional.
findings.evidence_en: The process for appointing lower federal judges: (1) Magistrate Council opens public competition, (2) administers written exam to qualified candidates, (3) evaluates personal background, (4) prepares binding shortlist of 3 candidates, (5) President selects from shortlist, (6) Senate confirms in public session. For Supreme Court justices, the process differs: President nominates, 2/3 Senate agreement required, with public hearings. In practice, Milei attempted to circumvent this via Decree 137/2025, appointing Lijo and Garcia-Mansilla 'in commission' without Senate agreement, which was declared unconstitutional.

findings.role: Federal Judge (Juzgado Federal 6, 1993-2020), retired July 2020 at age 75
findings.evidence_es: Canicoba Corral fue juez federal durante 27 anos, llegando al Juzgado Federal 6 en 1993 durante el gobierno de Menem. Presento renuncia y pidio jubilacion al acercarse a los 75 anos. En sus ultimos dias como juez, beneficio a kirchneristas y complico a macristas, incluyendo investigaciones contra el ex ministro de Hacienda Nicolas Dujovne en la causa Autopistas. Se jubilo en medio de acusaciones en el Consejo de la Magistratura por mal desempeno y presunta corrupcion, recibiendo una jubilacion de aproximadamente $330.000 mensuales. Habia manejado la causa AMIA. Su vacante en el Juzgado 6 permanecio sin cubrir por anos, siendo eventualmente absorbida por Lijo en 2023.
findings.evidence_en: Canicoba Corral was federal judge for 27 years, arriving at Federal Court 6 in 1993 during the Menem government. He resigned and requested retirement approaching age 75. In his final days as judge, he benefited Kirchnerists and complicated Macri allies, including investigations against former Finance Minister Nicolas Dujovne in the Autopistas case. He retired amid accusations before the Magistrate Council for poor performance and alleged corruption, receiving a retirement pension of approximately $330,000 pesos monthly. He had handled the AMIA case. His vacancy at Court 6 remained unfilled for years, eventually absorbed by Lijo in 2023.

new_entities.name: Rodolfo Canicoba Corral
new_entities.role: Former Federal Judge, Court 6 (1993-2020), Menem appointee, handled AMIA case

new_entities.name: Nicolas Dujovne
new_entities.role: Former Finance Minister investigated by Canicoba in Autopistas case

findings.role: Federal Judge, Court 10 — case concentration pattern
findings.evidence_es: Ercolini y el fiscal Taiano se han convertido en el duo que concentra las causas mas sensibles de Comodoro Py. Perfil titulo: 'Ercolini contra Alberto Fernandez y Lijo contra Macri: Comodoro Py se alinea con la agenda judicial de Milei'. Ademas de las causas Vialidad, Hotesur, Los Sauces y Oil Combustibles, Ercolini recibio la denuncia de Fabiola Yanez contra Alberto Fernandez por violencia de genero. Alberto Fernandez lo recuso en la causa Seguros, aportando chats entre ambos y declarando: 'Me detesta y me quiere preso'. Fue apartado de la causa 'Operativo Puff' por la Camara Federal. Designado en 2004 por Nestor Kirchner, termino siendo uno de los jueces que mas dano hizo al kirchnerismo.
findings.evidence_en: Ercolini and prosecutor Taiano have become the duo that concentrates the most sensitive cases at Comodoro Py. Perfil headlined: 'Ercolini vs Alberto Fernandez and Lijo vs Macri: Comodoro Py aligns with Milei's judicial agenda'. Beyond the Vialidad, Hotesur, Los Sauces and Oil Combustibles cases, Ercolini received Fabiola Yanez's complaint against Alberto Fernandez for gender violence. Alberto Fernandez recused him in the Insurance case, providing chats between them and declaring: 'He detests me and wants me in prison'. He was removed from 'Operativo Puff' case by the Federal Chamber. Appointed in 2004 by Nestor Kirchner, he ended up being one of the judges who most damaged Kirchnerism.

new_entities.name: Fabiola Yanez
new_entities.role: Former First Lady, filed gender violence complaint against Alberto Fernandez handled by Ercolini

findings.evidence_es: El Estudio O'Farrell, fundado en 1883 por Santiago Gregorio O'Farrell, es uno de los bufetes mas antiguos de Argentina con 140 anos de historia. Marval O'Farrell Mairal, con mas de 300 abogados, fue premiado como mejor estudio juridico de Argentina por Chambers and Partners en los Latin American Awards 2024 (12 veces, 7 consecutivas). La Ley de Etica Publica (25.188) establece incompatibilidades entre funcion publica y representacion de empresas reguladas por el Estado, pero nuestro grafo muestra jueces listados como directivos de empresas. La Oficina Anticorrupcion carece de mecanismos efectivos para fiscalizar estas incompatibilidades. Se requiere investigacion adicional para mapear las conexiones especificas entre estudios juridicos y jueces federales.
findings.evidence_en: The O'Farrell firm, founded in 1883 by Santiago Gregorio O'Farrell, is one of Argentina's oldest law firms with 140 years of history. Marval O'Farrell Mairal, with over 300 lawyers, was awarded best Argentine law firm by Chambers and Partners at the 2024 Latin American Awards (12 times, 7 consecutive). The Public Ethics Law (25.188) establishes incompatibilities between public office and representing state-regulated companies, but our graph shows judges listed as corporate officers. The Anti-Corruption Office lacks effective enforcement mechanisms. Additional investigation is needed to map specific connections between law firms and federal judges.

new_entities.name: Estudio O'Farrell
new_entities.description: One of Argentina's oldest law firms (est. 1883), specializing in complex legal matters

new_entities.name: Marval O'Farrell Mairal
new_entities.description: Argentina's largest law firm (300+ lawyers), awarded best firm 12 times by Chambers and Partners

findings.evidence_es: El 25 de febrero de 2025, Milei firmo el Decreto 137/2025 designando 'en comision' a Ariel Lijo y Manuel Garcia-Mansilla en la Corte Suprema. El juez Alejo Ramos Padilla declaro la inconstitucionalidad y no-convencionalidad del decreto. El juez afirmo que en los ultimos 100 anos ningun presidente electo democraticamente habia realizado nombramientos en comision a la Corte Suprema. El unico juez que asumio en comision en ese periodo fue el propio Garcia-Mansilla. El Senado habia rechazado expresamente el pliego de Lijo con 43 votos en contra. Expertos constitucionalistas criticaron la medida. El Observatorio del Derecho a la Ciudad logro la declaracion de inconstitucionalidad. El decreto viola el texto constitucional y 'afecta gravemente la forma republicana de gobierno, poniendo en riesgo la imparcialidad de los jueces y la independencia del Poder Judicial'.
findings.evidence_en: On February 25, 2025, Milei signed Decree 137/2025 appointing Ariel Lijo and Manuel Garcia-Mansilla to the Supreme Court 'in commission'. Judge Alejo Ramos Padilla declared the decree unconstitutional. The judge affirmed that in the last 100 years no democratically elected president had made temporary Supreme Court appointments. The only judge who assumed in commission in that period was Garcia-Mansilla himself. The Senate had expressly rejected Lijo's candidacy with 43 votes against. Constitutional experts criticized the measure. The Observatory of the Right to the City achieved the unconstitutionality declaration. The decree violates constitutional text and 'seriously affects the republican form of government, putting at risk judicial impartiality and the independence of the Judicial Branch'.

new_entities.name: Alejo Ramos Padilla
new_entities.role: Federal judge who declared Decree 137/2025 unconstitutional

new_entities.name: Observatorio del Derecho a la Ciudad
new_entities.description: Civil society org that brought the unconstitutionality action against Decree 137/2025

key_patterns: Case paralysis as political protection: Lijo kept 13 corruption cases open 10+ years without resolution, including Siemens (26 years), YPF (18 years), Ruckauf (17 years) — impunity through procedural delay

Unprecedented court concentration: Single judge (Lijo) controlled 3 of 12 federal courts simultaneously (25%), creating a de facto judicial oligopoly over federal criminal jurisdiction in Buenos Aires

Constitutional crisis via decree: Milei bypassed the constitutional 2/3 Senate requirement with Decree 137/2025, declared unconstitutional — first temporary Supreme Court appointment by a democratic president in 100 years
International condemnation: HRW, CELS, ACIJ, NBC News all documented systemic problems with Lijo nomination — Argentina's judicial independence crisis has international visibility

Menem's designed court: Comodoro Py itself was architecturally and institutionally designed by Carlos Menem in the 1990s, expanding from 6 to 12 courts and populating them with allied judges — the infrastructure of impunity is physical, not just procedural
Procuraduria vacante: The Attorney General position has been vacant since 2017, with an interim (Casal) lacking full democratic legitimacy — this weakens federal criminal prosecution at its root

Nisman-AMIA nexus: Rafecas dismissed Nisman's complaint against CFK without taking evidence, was sanctioned for 'lack of decorum', then was nominated as Attorney General by the same political faction — judicial favors creating career advancement
Judicial nepotism: Chequeado documented systemic kinship networks within Comodoro Py, perpetuating a closed system based on family connections over merit

Accusatory reform as power redistribution: The August 2025 shift from inquisitorial to accusatory system at Comodoro Py transfers investigative power from judges to prosecutors, fundamentally altering the ecosystem that enabled judges to selectively advance or delay cases
persons_identified: Daniel Eduardo Rafecas (Judge, Court 3, dismissed Nisman complaint)
Magali Mazzuca (Lijo's ex-partner, Supreme Court clerk)
Elisa Carrio (Former congresswoman, filed complaint against Lijo)
Amado Boudou (Former VP, processed by Lijo)
